revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}}
{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}}

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|12=draft}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=draft}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|12=draft}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=draft}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=draft}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=draft}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|12=draft}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=draft}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|12=draft}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=draft}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=draft}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=draft}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|12=draft}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=draft}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|12=draft}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=draft}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=draft}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=draft}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}

It is further stated:—"The names of the God ... {{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Hitorigami]] [[Category:Creator deities]] [[Category:Earth deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:国狭槌尊]] [[uk:%D0%9A%D1%83%D0%BD%D1%96 %D0%BD%D0%BE %D1%81%D0%B0%D1%86%D1%83%D1%82%D1%96 %D0%BD%D0%BE %D0%BC%D1%96%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE]] [[ru:%D0%9A%D1%83%D0%BD%D0%B8 %D1%81%D0%B0%D1%86%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B8-%D0%BD%D0%BE %D0%BC%D0%B8%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE]] [[zh:%E5%9C%8B%E7%8B%B9%E6%A7%8C%E5%B0%8A]] [[sn:Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]] [[ja:%E5%9B%BD%E7%8B%AD%E6%A7%8C%E5%B0%8A]] [[en:Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]]

It is further stated:—"The names of the God ... {{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Hitorigami]] [[Category:Creator deities]] [[Category:Earth deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]] [[Category:refreshing imports]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:国狭槌尊]] [[uk:%D0%9A%D1%83%D0%BD%D1%96 %D0%BD%D0%BE %D1%81%D0%B0%D1%86%D1%83%D1%82%D1%96 %D0%BD%D0%BE %D0%BC%D1%96%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE]] [[ru:%D0%9A%D1%83%D0%BD%D0%B8 %D1%81%D0%B0%D1%86%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B8-%D0%BD%D0%BE %D0%BC%D0%B8%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE]] [[zh:%E5%9C%8B%E7%8B%B9%E6%A7%8C%E5%B0%8A]] [[sn:Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]] [[ja:%E5%9B%BD%E7%8B%AD%E6%A7%8C%E5%B0%8A]] [[en:Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]]

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|12=draft}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=draft}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|12=draft}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=draft}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=draft}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=draft}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|12=draft}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=draft}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|12=draft}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=draft}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=draft}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=draft}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}

{{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} (Deity of the Land) ... 3 KB (363 words) - 22:05, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 7 KB (884 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 == See Also == *{{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日|12=draft}} – A deity associated with the introduction of rice cultivation to the Kinki region. * * [[Toyokumonu]]...之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} are followed by the third deity, {{ill|Toyoshinr ...之常立尊|lt=Kunini-no-Tokotachi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|lt=Kunikazutsu-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} as Toyokunushi-no-Mikoto (豊国主尊), also know ... 7 KB (723 words) - 17:56, 26 January 2025 * * [[Kamiyonanayo]]...inotokotachi-no-mikoto}}/ {{nihongo|[[Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-satsuchi]]|国狭槌尊, Kuni-no-satsuchi-no-mikoto}} ... 6 KB (658 words) - 12:42, 26 January 2025 * * [[Ryobu Shinto]]...okotachi|ja|国之常立神}} as the {{ill|Dharmakaya|ja|法身|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} as the {{ill|Sambhogakaya|ja|報身|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Toyokumono|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}} as the ... 7 KB (1,032 words) - 05:33, 26 January 2025 * * [[Aotsuka Kofun]] ...imes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (1,147 words) - 14:02, 26 January 2025 * [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] ...her, whose names were, first, Kuni-no-toko-tachi no Mikoto, and next [[:ja:国狭槌尊|Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]]." It is further stated:—"The names of the God ... {{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Hitorigami]] [[Category:Creator deities]] [[Category:Earth deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:国狭槌尊]] [[uk:%D0%9A%D1%83%D0%BD%D1%96 %D0%BD%D0%BE %D1%81%D0%B0%D1%86%D1%83%D1%82%D1%96 %D0%BD%D0%BE %D0%BC%D1%96%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE]] [[ru:%D0%9A%D1%83%D0%BD%D0%B8 %D1%81%D0%B0%D1%86%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B8-%D0%BD%D0%BE %D0%BC%D0%B8%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE]] [[zh:%E5%9C%8B%E7%8B%B9%E6%A7%8C%E5%B0%8A]] [[sn:Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]] [[ja:%E5%9B%BD%E7%8B%AD%E6%A7%8C%E5%B0%8A]] [[en:Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]]

revision.comment: 22 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:国狭槌尊 up to rev 104307544
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国狭槌尊 | 世代名 = [[神世七代]] 第2代 | 先代= [[国常立尊]] | 次代= [[豊斟渟尊]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[天津神]] | 全名 = 国狭槌尊 | 別名 = 国之狭土神、国狭立尊 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = [[熊野速玉大社]]、[[物部神社]]等 |画像=Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg|画像説明=（『神仏図会』）}}
[[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|「天地開闢」における神々（古事記による）]] '''国狭槌尊'''（くにさつちのみこと）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 主に『[[日本書紀]]』の[[天地開闢 (日本神話)|天地開闢]]の段に登場する神で別名'''国狭立尊'''（くにのさたちのみこと）。[[神代七代]]のうちの一柱である。
『[[古事記]]』には[[大山津見神]]の子に[[天之狭土神]]・国之狭土神がいるが、どちらも神代七代に数えられていない。
神名「サツチ」の「サ」は神稲、「ツチ」は土、即ち神稲を植える土の意か<ref>坂本・家永・井上・大野『日本書紀（一）』岩波文庫、補注1-九</ref>。
== 神話での記述 == 『日本書紀』本文では、天地開闢の後[[国常立尊]]、国狭槌尊が登場し、次に[[豊斟渟尊]]が化生したとしており、これらの三柱の神は男神であると記している。
第一、第二と第四の一書では、国常立尊の次に国狭槌尊が登場し、他の一書には同一神とみられる神名は登場しない。『日本書紀』にこれ以降、国狭槌尊が神話に登場することはない。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天地開闢 (日本神話)|天地開闢]] * [[神世七代]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[饒速日]] - 近畿地方に稲作を伝えた神
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くにのさつちのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:独神]] [[Category:創造神]] [[Category:大地神]] [[Category:天理教]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 国狭槌尊 | 世代名 = [[神世七代]] 第2代 | 先代= [[国常立尊]] | 次代= [[豊斟渟尊]] | 神祇 = <!-- 天神、地祇、天津神、国津神など -->[[天津神]] | 全名 = 国狭槌尊 | 別名 = 国之狭土神、国狭立尊 | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = <!-- 太陽、月、山、海など司るもの --> | 陵所 = | 父 = | 母 = | 親 = <!-- まぐあいによって生まれない場合 --> | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = [[熊野速玉大社]]、[[物部神社]]等 |画像=Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg|画像説明=（『神仏図会』）}}
[[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|「天地開闢」における神々（古事記による）]] '''国狭槌尊'''（くにさつちのみこと）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 主に『[[日本書紀]]』の[[天地開闢 (日本神話)|天地開闢]]の段に登場する神で別名'''国狭立尊'''（くにのさたちのみこと）。[[神代七代]]のうちの一柱である。
『[[古事記]]』には[[大山津見神]]の子に[[天之狭土神]]・国之狭土神がいるが、どちらも神代七代に数えられていない。
神名「サツチ」の「サ」は神稲、「ツチ」は土、即ち神稲を植える土の意か<ref>坂本・家永・井上・大野『日本書紀（一）』岩波文庫、補注1-九</ref>。
== 神話での記述 == 『日本書紀』本文では、天地開闢の後[[国常立尊]]、国狭槌尊が登場し、次に[[豊斟渟尊]]が化生したとしており、これらの三柱の神は男神であると記している。
第一、第二と第四の一書では、国常立尊の次に国狭槌尊が登場し、他の一書には同一神とみられる神名は登場しない。『日本書紀』にこれ以降、国狭槌尊が神話に登場することはない。
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[天地開闢 (日本神話)|天地開闢]] * [[神世七代]] * [[日本の神の一覧]] * [[饒速日]] - 近畿地方に稲作を伝えた神
{{日本神話}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くにのさつちのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:独神]] [[Category:創造神]] [[Category:大地神]] [[Category:天理教]]

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ}}

It is further stated:—"The names of the God ... {{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Amatsukami]] [[Category:Hitorigami]] [[Category:Creator deities]] [[Category:Earth deities]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:国狭槌尊]] [[uk:%D0%9A%D1%83%D0%BD%D1%96 %D0%BD%D0%BE %D1%81%D0%B0%D1%86%D1%83%D1%82%D1%96 %D0%BD%D0%BE %D0%BC%D1%96%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE]] [[ru:%D0%9A%D1%83%D0%BD%D0%B8 %D1%81%D0%B0%D1%86%D1%83%D1%82%D0%B8-%D0%BD%D0%BE %D0%BC%D0%B8%D0%BA%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE]] [[zh:%E5%9C%8B%E7%8B%B9%E6%A7%8C%E5%B0%8A]] [[sn:Kuni no sa-tsuchi no Mikoto]] [[ja:%E5%9B%BD%E7%8B%AD%E6%A7%8C%E5%B0%8A]] [[en:Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]] {{translated page|ja|国狭槌尊|version=104307544|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|12=draft}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=draft}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|12=draft}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=draft}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=draft}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=draft}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|12=draft}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=draft}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=draft}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|12=draft}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|12=draft}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|12=draft}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|12=draft}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=draft}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=draft}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|12=draft}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=draft}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=draft}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=draft}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|qq=}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese mythology|qq=}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese creation myth|qq=}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国常立尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|qq=}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}

Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|qq=}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|qq=}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese mythology|qq=}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese creation myth|qq=}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|qq=}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgoshajinja (Chino City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|qq=}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese mythology|qq=}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese creation myth|qq=}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|qq=}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}
{{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|qq=}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese mythology|qq=}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese creation myth|qq=}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|qq=}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}

After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}

{{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami|qq=}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qq=}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|qq=}} and others }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kami|qq=}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese mythology|qq=}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|ja|天地開闢 (日本神話)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese creation myth|qq=}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|ja|神世七代|1=Kamiyonanayo|2=ja|3=神世七代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamiyonanayo|qq=}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|qq=}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|ja|国之常立神|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|2=ja|3=国之常立神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|qq=}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|qq=}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]] * [[Omuro Sengen Shrine (Ōasomi, Fujiyoshida)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Settsu)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|ja|ミヅハノメ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanome|qq=}}
