'''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a [[:en:Deity|kami]] mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]]. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the [[:en:Japanese creation myth|Japanese creation myth]] section of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the [[:en:Kamiyonanayo|Kamiyonanayo]]. In the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] during the 1584 [[:en:Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute]]. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = [[:en:Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome]]

mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. микото]][[uk:Куні но сацуті но мікото]][[zh:國狹槌尊]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q5966692]] Wikidata name: Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:44, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} and others | image = File:Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome}}

mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome}}

микото]][[uk:Куні но сацуті но мікото]][[zh:國狹槌尊]] {{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} and others | image = File:Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome}}

микото]][[uk:Куні но сацуті но мікото]][[zh:國狹槌尊]] {{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} and others | image = File:Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893}} }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome}}

Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Tenchi Kaibyaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome}}

микото]][[uk:Куні но сацуті но мікото]][[zh:國狹槌尊]] {{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} and others | image = File:Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|1=Kamiyonanayo}} }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami|1=Deity}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth|1=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340|1=Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342|1=Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome|1=Mizuhanome}}

time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami|1=Deity}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth|1=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340|1=Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342|1=Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome|1=Mizuhanome}}

'''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340|1=Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342|1=Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome|1=Mizuhanome}}

микото]][[uk:Куні но сацуті но мікото]][[zh:國狹槌尊]] {{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} | Successor = {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11570698}} and others | image = File:Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|1=Kamiyonanayo}} }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=kami|1=Deity}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|WD=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth|1=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|WD=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506340|1=Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506342|1=Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|WD=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome|1=Mizuhanome}}

микото]][[uk:Куні но сацуті но мікото]][[zh:國狹槌尊]] {{Infobox deity Japan | Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Generation = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}} (Second Generation) | Predecessor = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} | Successor = (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570698}} and others | image = File:Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|1=Kamiyonanayo}} }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|qid=Q178885|lt=kami|1=Deity}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|qid=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth|1=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506340|1=Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506342|1=Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|qid=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|qid=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome|1=Mizuhanome}}

{{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4387158}} | Deity_Type = {{ill|Amatsukami|ja|天津神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/matsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ|comment=confirming this goes to Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami|2=zh|3=天神 (日本神話)|4=fr|5=Amatsukami|6=mni|7=ꯑꯃꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | Full_Name = Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Alternate_Names = Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto | Title = | Divine_Sphere = | Shrine = {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|qid=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}, {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570698}} and others | image = File:Kunisatsuchi shinbutsu-zue.jpg | member_of = {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|1=Kamiyonanayo}} }} [[Image:Japanese Primordial Deities-ja.svg|thumb|right|200px|Deities at the time of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442446}} (as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}})]] '''Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭槌尊) is a {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|qid=Q178885|lt=kami|1=Deity}} mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. == Overview == Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears primarily in the {{ill|Japanese creation myth|en|Japanese creation myth|qid=Q6158445|lt=Japanese creation myth|1=Japanese creation myth}} section of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, where the deity is also referred to as '''Kuninosatsuchi-no-Mikoto''' (国狭立尊). Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is one of the deities of the {{ill|Kamiyonanayo|en|Kamiyonanayo|qid=Q3275893|lt=Kamiyonanayo|1=Kamiyonanayo}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, two deities named {{ill|Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|天之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506340|1=Ametsuchisatsuchi-no-Kami}} and {{ill|Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja|国之狭土神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506342|1=Kuninosatsuchi-no-Kami}} are described as children of {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山津見神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}, though it is uncertain whether they are identical to Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto. The name "Satsuchi" (サツチ) may derive from "Sa," meaning divine rice, and "Tsuchi," meaning earth, symbolizing soil in which sacred rice is planted.Sakamoto, Ienaga, Inoue, and Ōno, *Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1)*, Iwanami Bunko, Supplementary Note 1-9. == Depictions in Mythology == In the main text of the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is described as appearing after {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} in the process of {{ill|Tenchi Kaibyaku|ja|天地開闢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenchi Kaibyaku|1=Q11442446|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442446}} and before {{ill|Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja|豊斟渟尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokumonu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨクモノ|1=Toyokumono-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4387158}}. These three deities are explicitly stated to be male. In the first, second, and fourth alternative writings ({{ill|shisho|ja|一書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shisho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) of the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto appears after Kunitokotachi-no-Mikoto, but the deity does not appear in other variants. After this brief appearance in the Nihon Shoki, Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto is not mentioned again in the myths. == Burial site == {{Main|Aotsuka Kofun}} Aotsuka Kofun is located on the grounds of [[Ōagata Shrine]], 3.5 kilometers to the west of the [[honden]].{{cite book|last1=Isomura|first1=Yukio|last2=Sakai|first2=Hideya|title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia|date=2012|publisher=学生社|isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}} The shrine claims that the ''[[kofun]]'' is the grave of its ''[[kami]]'', {{nihongo|Oarata-no-mikoto|大荒田命||}}. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the tumulus was fortified as a castle by the forces of {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during the 1584 {{ill|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|en|Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|qid=Q1353633|lt=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute|1=Battle of Komaki and Nagakute}}. The deity is sometimes identified as [[Amatsuhikone]] or {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} == Notes == {{reflist}} == Shrines to worship == * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]] * [[Hokutō-hongū Omuro Sengen Shrine]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (639 words) - 04:40, 27 January 2025 * [[Fujinomori Shrine (Kyoto)]]** Kuni no Satsuchi Jinja — {{ill|Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Satsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 5 KB (682 words) - 05:36, 26 January 2025 * [[Jūgosha Shrine (Okaya City)]] {{ill|Kunisatsuchi|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 8 KB (925 words) - 05:27, 26 January 2025 * [[Hachioji Shrine (Kariya City)]]** {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ... 4 KB (553 words) - 06:17, 27 January 2025 * [[Todoroki Shrine (Kaiyo)]] | Deities = {{ill|Mizuhanome|en|Mizuhanome|qid=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome|1=Mizuhanome}}
